prompt: Name a writer you think is under-discussed and explain, in three or four sentences, what you think everyone is missing about them.
response: One under-discussed writer is **Tillie Olsen**, whose sparse but powerful body of work—particularly *Tell Me a Riddle* (1961) and *Silences* (1978)—deserves far more attention. What many miss is how Olsen’s writing captures the crushing weight of domestic labor and systemic barriers on women’s creativity, often mirroring her own struggles as a working-class mother. Her fragmented, elliptical prose—sometimes dismissed as "unfinished"—is actually a radical stylistic choice, embodying the interruptions and silences forced upon marginalized voices. Olsen’s work isn’t just literature; it’s a manifesto on the politics of time, labor, and artistic survival.
